verdict: SPLIT-BRAIN RESOLVED — single source of truth restored

root_cause.summary: pinterest-recovery-orchestrator selected columns that do not exist on public.pinterest_connection (access_token_expires_at, last_health_check, last_health_status). PostgREST returned an error, conn was null, and the orchestrator therefore reported oauth_unhealthy_or_missing while the actual OAuth token was healthy and verified against Pinterest (account 200, boards 200, 32 boards, expires 2026-07-26).
root_cause.secondary: scopes column is TEXT (space-separated), not array. Array.isArray() always false → missing_scopes:catalogs,ads always reported regardless of grant.

auto_repairs: Patched supabase/functions/pinterest-recovery-orchestrator/index.ts to read correct columns (token_expires_at, last_account_status, last_boards_status, scopes as text).
Added oauth_source telemetry to phase_1 output so every future run logs token fingerprint, account, expiry, and live Pinterest API verification status.
Re-ran orchestrator end-to-end. Blocker oauth_unhealthy_or_missing cleared.

blockers_after_fix.reason: Pinterest requires interactive user consent for new scopes. No server-side API path can grant them.
Trust improves only by publishing new premium pins — gated by missing scopes + safety locks.

human_actions_required: Visit /admin/pinterest → Reconnect with Full Access, granting catalogs:* and ads:* scopes during Pinterest consent.
After re-OAuth, the same row in pinterest_connection is overwritten and the next orchestrator run will auto-clear the missing_scopes blocker.
